positives: Top card is a real Futian independent izakaya 鹤松·居酒屋 福田灯光秀店 — exactly the kind of non-chain late-night place a heartbroken PM would trust; verb-chip '到 02:00' answers 'can I stay past midnight?' in one glance

'暖光' (warm light) chip is the verbatim comfort signal Chen Manqing came looking for — not the word 'cozy', a specific quality she can picture; Solo 9.5 + Best now feels like a friend saying 'go here tonight, it fits you'
specific_fixes: The persona would feel 100% seen if a 一人吧台 (solo counter) chip were also shown on this top card alongside 暖光/到 02:00 — the second seed 旧天堂书店 already carries 单人吧台, so promote that vocabulary to the izakaya card too

positives: No 7-Eleven / no 全家 anywhere — the chain-food failure mode is cleanly avoided even at 01:00

No 'Explore Chiang Mai' upsell for a hungry tired traveler — the app respects his city and hour, and the top card carries '到 02:00' which is at least the right kind of information for someone landing at 01:00
failure_modes_triggered: ChatCard 缺营业时间字段 — the ChatSheet three-card 粥铺/大排档/24h 面馆 layout the rubric expects never appears; user is dropped into the same Nearby list as noon, and top card is an izakaya at 15.6 km, not a 5-minute-walk 粥铺
specific_fixes: At scenarioHour ∈ [0,3] and city=szx, force a midnight-food ChatSheet triad (粥铺 / 大排档 / 24h 面馆) at ≤2 km with '距离 · 步行 X min · 营业到 X' on a single row — right now a delayed dad at 1am is offered a 15 km izakaya, which is technically open but emotionally wrong

positives: Gammeeok — 24h Korean seolleongtang, Koreatown with 'counter seat' chip and $16 — a socially-anxious solo drinker can walk in, sit at the counter, be one of many, not the object of attention; this is the exact 'you can sit at the far end of the counter' verb the rubric asks for

Sake Bar Decibel (East 9th St basement) — the parenthetical 'East 9th St basement' does the work of ten adjectives: she knows it's below street, quiet, hidden; 'counter seat' chip stacks on top; Housing Works Bookstore Café as the third option is exactly the kind of non-bar decompression she might need
specific_fixes: One small caring add: promote a 'no-pressure staff' or 'bartender speaks softly' chip on Sake Bar Decibel alongside 'counter seat' — the persona's real fear is being watched, and naming the bartender's behavior would earn the last 3 points

positives: The 'Explore Chiang Mai' tone-deaf CTA that broke round 25 is GONE — a grieving 52-year-old woman at 5am is no longer asked to consider a different country. This alone earns emotional_resonance

Meiji Jingu inner path, dawn walk with 'flat walk · benches' chips is exactly what a grieving woman needs — a path to walk, benches when she needs to sit, no conversation required; Aoyama Book Center Café corner ¥550 top card gives her a warm indoor kissaten alternative — a real name, not 'a lovely traditional cafe'
specific_fixes: Add a 'no conversation needed' or '静か' chip on the Aoyama Book Center card — the -seniorPersona + 5am grief context deserves a chip that says 'you can be silent here' in one word, so Marta doesn't have to guess

positives: 'Ritual Coffee Roasters, Valencia St — rainy day back tables' — 'rainy day back tables' is a specific verb-image only a friend who knows the shop would say; it acknowledges Kwame's rainy-day anxiety AND tells him where to sit. Perfect emotional register for a man dodging weather

'City Lights Bookstore + Vesuvio Café next door' — the '+ next door' phrase treats him like an intelligent adult navigating two adjacent spaces, and 'Hog Island Oyster Co. — Ferry Building bar counter (weekday)' gives him the exact seat + day-of-week context. No Fisherman's Wharf trap
specific_fixes: Add explicit 'indoor-only · wifi · power-outlet' chips on the Ritual and City Lights cards — the writing already implies indoor comfort, but a Londoner soaking wet wants to see those three chips flash in one glance

positives: 'The Workshop Coffee rooftop — District 1/3 border' with 'no cover' chip and đ85k — a working photographer reads this in one second: rooftop = sky, D1/3 border = accessible, no cover = walk in with gear, price known. Verb-first, adjective-free

'Okkio Caffe (D3, Vo Van Tan) — women-solo-frequent evenings' — even though this persona is a working photographer not a nervous woman, the app quietly shows she can trust the neighborhood; no '祝你拍得开心' cheerleader tone anywhere
failure_modes_triggered: BestTimeRibbon 未按实际日落校准 — no BestTimeRibbon rendered at all on the settled screen; a golden-hour photographer opens the app at 17:00 and cannot see when the light will actually break
specific_fixes: For scenarioHour ∈ [16,18] and city=sgn, render BestTimeRibbon prominently with local sunset time (≈18:12 in July) and a 'west-facing' chip on the Workshop Coffee rooftop card — the persona's entire reason for launching the app is a time window, and it must be visible in one glance

positives: Now filter shows 1 result — a decision-fatigued analyst doesn't want 20 options; the reduction respects his 45-minute clock
% Arabica 前海嘉里中心 with 空调 (AC) chip is at least the right vocabulary for a 34°C 深圳 noon — the persona's central chip signal is present, just not on a route card
failure_modes_triggered: Route 总时长 >45min — no Route card exists; the -lunchQuick flag does not produce a 45-minute office → AC lunch → return loop; distances shown (13.7 km) alone would blow the budget
specific_fixes: -lunchQuick + city=szx must synthesize a 45-min Route card in Now with three stops (office → AC-strong lunch → return), each carrying '空调 · 步行 X min · ¥XX' on one row; the persona should never have to add up the loop in his head at his desk

positives: 'Tasca do Chico, Bairro Alto — small-room fado, no cover, no mi[nimum]' at €4 — this line does more for a 20-year-old first-time cross-border traveler than any onboarding: a real named tasca, the neighborhood, what she'll actually experience (small-room fado), and the two words that protect her wallet ('no cover'). Zero adjectives, three verbs

'Tasca Zé dos Cornos, Alfama — grilled sardines, cash only' at €10 with 'counter seat' chip — 'cash only' is exactly the hard-cap-budget signal the rubric demands, and 'grilled sardines' is a concrete dish not 'traditional Portuguese fare'. Livraria Bertrand Chiado 'café at the back' as third option shows the app knows she also wants a quiet reading corner
specific_fixes: This is the story that hits 100 on my card. Only nitpick: promote a 'local-frequent' chip alongside 'no cover' on Tasca do Chico so the tourist-trap-avoidance signal is a chip and not just a sentence — but the sentence already carries it

positives: 'Meiji Jingu inner path, dawn walk' with 'flat walk · benches' chips — this is EXACTLY the two-chip signature the rubric names 'the soul' of this persona; a 68-year-old widower on his first solo trip sees in one glance that he can walk without climbing and sit when his knees ask. Coffee Ron Shibuya west exit at 326m / ~4 min is the perfect low-effort backup

No 'Explore Chiang Mai', no 'partner up' tone, no '爷爷' pandering — the app treats Robert as an intelligent traveler carrying grief, which is the correct register. Aoyama Book Center at ¥550 gives him a warm indoor option that respects his budget without being patronizing
specific_fixes: Add an 'english signage' or 'staff speaks English' chip on Meiji Jingu — the rubric names it as one of three key chips ('flat-walk / benches-available / english-signage'); the first two are shown, the third would earn 100 clean

positives: The Explore overlay resolves into a real card with the sentence 'Well-lit café-bar on Vo Van Tan open to 23:00, Grab drops at door, solo women regulars' — every clause is a verb ('drops at door') or a concrete fact ('open to 23:00'); this is the rubric's most-wanted line for the whole app. A young woman worried about walking home at night gets three specific safety verbs in one breath

The half-transparent 'Scanning · Ho Chi Minh · 5 km · searching…' overlay + Cancel does not obscure her District 3 — she watches the app work FOR her while still seeing her own city; then the result lands with the girl-avatar 'Ask' companion — feels like being walked home by a friend, not surveilled by a system
specific_fixes: One small chip-level polish: extract 'well-lit-street · grab-easy-return · women-solo-frequent' from the sentence into three chips on the card so the safety signals are scannable at a glance, not read; the words are already there in prose
